mesh.title: Mesh
mesh.subtitle: Connect your services and devices with post-quantum encrypted mesh networking. Mesh allows you to route traffic privately without VPNs or bastion hosts.
mesh.no_nodes_description: Nodes are servers, VMs, or containers that you connect via the CLI. Once online, any device running the Cloudflare One Client — your laptop, phone, or another node — can reach them and each other over a private, encrypted mesh.
mesh.common_use_cases_subtitle: Learn how mesh networking fits into your infrastructure.
mesh.tutorial.connect_client_description: Install the Cloudflare One Client and start reaching your mesh nodes from any device.
mesh.tutorial.replace_vpn_description: Migrate from legacy VPN to Cloudflare for faster, more secure access.
mesh.tutorial.connectivity_options_descr: Explore ways to connect your networks, services, and devices to Cloudflare.
mesh.table.name: Name
mesh.table.no_results_description: Try adjusting your search or filters.
mesh.delete.title: Delete node
mesh.delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete %{nodeName}? This will disconnect the node from your mesh network and revoke its credentials. This action cannot be undone.
mesh.usage.title: Usage
mesh.map.add_nodes: Add nodes to build your mesh network
mesh.add.title: Add node
mesh.add.team_setup.label: Choose a team name
mesh.add.team_setup.title: Choose a team name
mesh.add.team_setup.description: Before creating mesh nodes, you need a Cloudflare One team name. Your team will use this name to connect devices to your mesh network.
mesh.add.team_setup.info: Your team name creates a unique login URL for your organization. Team members and devices will use %{teamDomain} to authenticate and join your mesh network.
mesh.add.team_setup.error_too_short: Team name must be at least 2 characters.
mesh.add.team_setup.error_invalid: Use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Must start and end with a letter or number.
mesh.add.team_setup.error_default: Failed to set up your organization. Please try again.
mesh.add.name_description: Give your node a descriptive name so you can identify it in your mesh network.
mesh.add.name_error_empty: Please enter a name for your node.
mesh.add.name_error_too_short: Name must be at least 2 characters.
mesh.add.name_error_invalid: Name must start with a letter or number and can contain letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, and underscores.
mesh.add.creating_description: Setting up your mesh node on Cloudflare's network.
mesh.add.create_failed: Failed to create node. Please try again.
mesh.add.enrollment.label: Who can connect
mesh.add.enrollment.title: Who can connect to your mesh?
mesh.add.enrollment.description: Choose who is allowed to enroll devices on your mesh network. You can change this later in device enrollment settings.
mesh.add.enrollment.my_org_description: Anyone with an @%{domain} email can enroll devices.
mesh.add.enrollment.hint: You can update this anytime in
mesh.add.progress.label: Setting up
mesh.add.progress.title: Setting up your mesh
mesh.add.progress.description: Configuring your account for mesh networking. This only takes a moment.
mesh.add.install_description: Run these commands on %{nodeName} to join it to your mesh. This CLI setup is for servers and virtual machines (Ubuntu/Debian). To connect a laptop or phone, use the Cloudflare One Client instead.
mesh.add.provisioning_warning: Some settings could not be configured automatically:
mesh.add.warning.icmp_proxy: Could not enable ICMP proxy for ping support.
mesh.add.warning.traffic_proxy: Could not enable traffic proxying (TCP, UDP, and ICMP).
mesh.add.warning.virtual_ip: Could not assign virtual IP addresses to devices.
mesh.add.warning.default_profile: Could not update the default device profile to allow mesh traffic. Client devices may not be able to reach mesh nodes.
mesh.add.warning.split_tunnel: Could not configure the mesh device profile's traffic routing. Mesh nodes may not be able to reach each other.
mesh.add.warning.device_profile: Could not create the mesh device profile.
mesh.add.warning.enrollment: Could not configure device enrollment permissions.
mesh.add.profile_configured: We configured a device profile for your mesh nodes and updated your default profile to route mesh traffic. Only mesh (CGNAT) IPs are tunneled — your nodes' public IPs remain reachable.
mesh.add.success_description: Your mesh node is online and part of your mesh. Other nodes and devices can reach this node at the IP address below.
mesh.add.success_skipped_description: Your mesh node is created but not connected. Run the install commands on your server to bring it online. Once connected, you can reach it from other nodes, or from any device running the Cloudflare One Client.
mesh.add.mesh_ip_reach_description: Other nodes and devices on your mesh can reach this node at:
mesh.add.test_connectivity_description: Run from another device on your mesh to verify the connection.
mesh.add.second_node_description: Add another server or VM using the same CLI setup. Nodes communicate privately over their mesh IPs.
mesh.add.connect_from_node_description: Your %{count} nodes can reach each other using their mesh IPs.
mesh.add.access_from_device: Access from your laptop or phone
mesh.add.access_from_device_description: Download the Cloudflare One Client (available for macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android), sign in to your organization, and toggle it on. Your device automatically joins the mesh and can reach any node by its mesh IP - no CLI needed.
mesh.detail.not_found: Node not found. It may have been deleted.
mesh.detail.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete %{nodeName}? This action cannot be undone.
mesh.error.duplicate_name: A node with this name already exists. Please choose a different name.
mesh.error.invalid_name: This name is not valid. Use letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, or dots.
mesh.error.rate_limit: Too many requests. Please wait a moment and try again.
mesh.error.unauthorized: You do not have permission to perform this action.
mesh.error.create_node: Failed to create node. Please try again.
mesh.error.delete_node: Failed to delete node. Please try again.
mesh.error.rename_node: Failed to rename node. Please try again.
mesh.error.team_name_taken: This team name is already taken. Please choose a different one.
mesh.error.org_setup: Failed to set up your organization. Please try again.
mesh.detail.install_description: Run these commands on %{nodeName} to bring it online and join your mesh.
mesh.detail.reconnect_description: %{nodeName} is currently offline. Run these commands on the server to reconnect it.
mesh.detail.install_replica_description: Run these commands to connect a server or virtual machine as a node. This is helpful when reinstalling the agent or adding replicas.
mesh.detail.speed_test_default: Speed test results and live network metrics
mesh.detail.capture_history_description: Past diagnostics, packet captures, and speed test runs for this node.
mesh.detail.no_captures: No captures yet. Run a diagnostic to get started.
mesh.detail.install_commands_description: Run on a server or VM to connect it as this node. Useful for reinstalling the agent or adding replicas.
mesh.detail.test_connectivity_descriptio: Run from another device on your mesh.
mesh.detail.diagnostics_description: Run remote diagnostics on this node.
mesh.detail.diagnostics_not_eligible: This device is not eligible for remote diagnostics. Ensure the agent is connected and up to date.
mesh.detail.rename_too_long: Node name must be 255 characters or fewer.
mesh.detail.add_route_description: Add a private network route that will be accessible through this node. Traffic to this destination will be routed through the node.
mesh.detail.route_network_invalid: Enter a valid CIDR range (e.g. 10.0.0.0/24 or fd00::/48).
mesh.detail.edit_route_description: Update the network CIDR or description for this route.
mesh.detail.delete_route_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the route %{network}? Traffic to this network will no longer be routed through this node.
mesh.detail.speed.download_speed_tooltip: The maximum download throughput measured during the speed test.
mesh.detail.speed.upload_speed_tooltip: The maximum upload throughput measured during the speed test.
mesh.detail.speed.download_latency_toolt: Latency measured while downloading data under load.
mesh.detail.speed.upload_latency_tooltip: Latency measured while uploading data under load.
mesh.detail.speed.packet_loss_tooltip: Percentage of data packets lost during the speed test.
mesh.detail.ha_tooltip: Deploy multiple replicas with automatic failover. Set at creation time.
mesh.detail.ha_badge_tooltip: Running multiple replicas with automatic failover
mesh.detail.promote_description: This replica is on standby. Promote it to start routing traffic through it.
mesh.detail.promote_confirm_description: All traffic will be rerouted through %{deviceName}. The current active replica will switch to passive. This takes effect immediately.
mesh.detail.promote_confirm_warning: Failover is immediate. Existing connections to the current active replica will be re-established through the new active replica.
mesh.detail.promote_confirm: Promote this replica to active? The current active replica will become passive.
mesh.detail.name: Name
mesh.detail.no_recent_diagnostics: No diagnostics have been run yet.
mesh.detail.connect_your_node_descriptio: Run these commands on %{nodeName} to join it to your mesh.
mesh.detail.diagnostic_logs_description: Generate diagnostic logs that you can use to troubleshoot connectivity issues.
mesh.detail.speed_test_description: Measure the speed and consistency of device connection to the internet.
mesh.detail.routes_description: Add private network routes so devices on your mesh can reach hosts behind this node — even if those hosts aren't running the agent.
mesh.detail.diagnostics_page_description: Collect network data for real-time traffic monitoring and troubleshooting.
mesh.detail.route_network_help: Enter an IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR range (e.g. 10.0.0.0/24)
mesh.detail.add_hostname_route_descripti: Map a private hostname to this mesh node. Devices on your mesh can resolve this hostname to reach services behind the node.
mesh.detail.edit_hostname_route_descript: Update the hostname or description for this private hostname route.
mesh.detail.delete_hostname_route_confir: Are you sure you want to delete the private hostname %{hostname}? It will no longer resolve through this node.
mesh.detail.add_route_type_description: Choose the type of route to add to this node.
mesh.detail.route_type_cidr_description: Route traffic for a private network range through this node.
mesh.detail.route_type_hostname_descript: Map a private hostname to this node so devices can resolve it.
mesh.tutorial.team_name_not_configured: Your team name is configured when you create your first mesh node. Go to Mesh > Add node to get started — you'll set it in the first step.
mesh.tutorial.node_hint_online: Your node **%{nodeName}** is online at `%{nodeIp}` — try it now:
mesh.tutorial.node_hint_offline: You can find each node's Mesh IP on the dashboard or on any node detail page.
